$comment: c18 (positional macro expansion, C 6.10.3) runtime witness: a `#define name VALUE` must NOT retroactively replace a use of `name` that appears BEFORE it. SQLite's omit pattern declares a name then `#define`s it to a constant; the declaration must survive (else `int base_val;` becomes `int 40;` -> parse error -> no binary). The uses in the call args (after the #defines) expand: add(base_val,margin_val) -> add(40,2) = 42 -> exit 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: pre-c18 the whole body expanded with the final table, clobbering the earlier declarations -> build failure. base_val/margin_val reach add() as function args so the baseline arm keeps a live runtime add (not const-folded), and the optimizedPipelines `release` arm runs the shipped pipeline over the same source.
